fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: High-growth potential company with a premium valuation, currently unprofitable but with strong analyst estimates for future earnings.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY, with analyst estimates for current Q and FY showing substantial expected increases.
Forward P/E of 35.42, while high, is supported by a PEG ratio of 0.78, suggesting growth may justify the multiple.
Strong balance sheet with a current ratio of 3.83 and quick ratio of 2.85, indicating good short-term liquidity.
Total cash of $439.70M exceeds total debt of $280.42M.
negatives: Operating margin of -8.6% and profit margin of -8.5% indicate the company is currently unprofitable.
Return on equity of -6.1% and return on assets of -3.3% show poor returns on capital.
Free cash flow (TTM) is negative at $-445.58M, indicating cash burn.
Debt/Equity ratio of 25.36 is high, though offset by strong current assets.
Last reported quarter missed EPS estimates by 44.8%.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) has seen significant positive attention recently, with articles highlighting substantial transceiver orders and impressive stock performance, up over 600% in six months. Despite the strong upward momentum and analyst growth estimates, the company is currently unprofitable on a trailing basis, with negative margins and free cash flow. The narrative appears to be shifting towards future growth potential, though the valuation and current profitability remain key points of discussion.
